markdownRemark.html:
When using the CodeArea component directly, the default theme is relatively plain, lacking features like code folding and syntax highlighting. By importing built-in theme resources, you can make the component's style more enriched and switch between different themes for different language environments.

Built-in Themes

The CodeArea component has three built-in themes: neat, idea, and material, with neat as the default theme, as shown below:

  • Neat Theme
  • Idea Theme
  • Material Theme

You can switch themes by configuring the option property of the CodeArea component:

<CodeArea
  defaultValue={jsText}
  options={{
    theme: "idea", // Change the theme by modifying this property
  }}
/>

However, these themes may not always meet your styling needs. In such cases, you need to import CodeArea theme resources to enrich your editor styles.

Referencing Theme Resources

By default, CodeArea does not include all resource files, so we can import theme resources as needed.

Main resources to import as needed:

  1. Theme style files
  2. Language modules
  3. Highlighting plugins
  4. Fold plugins

Importing Theme Style Files

By importing theme style files, the main colors and fonts of the editor will be modified.

import 'codemirror/theme/yonce.css';
import 'codemirror/theme/lucario.css';
import 'codemirror/theme/monokai.css';

Here, three theme style files are imported. The current theme can be set using the theme property in the CodeArea option configuration.

const option = {
  theme: 'yonce',
}

More theme packages can be found in the node_modules/codemirror/theme folder.

For more available theme styles, please refer to: CodeMirror Themes

Importing Language Modules

By importing language mode files, different code styles will be applied according to the language type.

// You can choose different modes based on the corresponding language
import "codemirror/mode/htmlmixed/htmlmixed"
import "codemirror/mode/htmlembedded/htmlembedded"
import "codemirror/mode/javascript/javascript"

Here, three language modules are imported, and the current language type is set through the mode property in the option configuration of CodeArea.

const option = {
  mode: { name: 'text/javascript' },
}

More language packages can be found in the node_modules/codemirror/mode folder.

For more language configuration options, please refer to: CodeMirror Language

Import Cursor Highlight Plugin

By importing the cursor highlight plugin, it is used to highlight the code line where the cursor is located.

import 'codemirror/addon/selection/active-line';

After importing, you can manually enable it in the option configuration.

const option = {
 styleActiveLine: true, // Highlight the code line of the cursor
}

More highlight plugin packages can be found in the node_modules/codemirror/addon/selection folder.

For more highlight plugin configurations, please refer to: CodeMirror Highlight

Import Code Folding Plugin

By importing the code folding plugin, it provides commands and other features related to code folding (such as temporarily hiding code blocks).

import 'codemirror/addon/fold/foldgutter.css'; 
import 'codemirror/addon/fold/foldcode.js';
import 'codemirror/addon/fold/foldgutter.js';
import 'codemirror/addon/fold/brace-fold.js';
import 'codemirror/addon/fold/comment-fold.js';

After importing, you need to manually enable folding and configure the fold type in the option settings.

const option = {
  foldGutter: true, // Enable folding
  gutters: ['CodeMirror-linenumbers', 'CodeMirror-foldgutter'], // Fold types
}

You can find more folding addons in the node_modules/codemirror/addon/fold folder.

For more folding configuration, please refer to: CodeMirror Folder

Complete code

import React from 'react';
import { CodeArea } from 'choerodon-ui/pro';

// Import theme style file
import 'codemirror/theme/yonce.css';
// import 'codemirror/theme/lucario.css';
// import 'codemirror/theme/monokai.css';
// Introduce language module
import "codemirror/mode/javascript/javascript"
// import "codemirror/mode/htmlmixed/htmlmixed"
// import "codemirror/mode/htmlembedded/htmlembedded"
//Code Highlighting
import 'codemirror/addon/selection/active-line';
// Code folding
import 'codemirror/addon/fold/foldgutter.css';
import 'codemirror/addon/fold/foldgutter.js';
import 'codemirror/addon/fold/brace-fold.js';
// import 'codemirror/addon/fold/foldcode.js';
// import 'codemirror/addon/fold/comment-fold.js';

export default const App = () => {
  // Configure the option parameters according to requirements
 return (
    <CodeArea
      style={{ height: 400, width: 400 }}
      options={{
        lineNumbers: true, // Show line numbers
        mode: { name: 'text/javascript' }, // Language
        autofocus: true, // Automatically get focus
        styleActiveLine: true, // Cursor code highlighting
        theme: "yonce", // Theme
        lineWrapping: true, // Automatic line wrap for code
        foldGutter: true, // Allow folding
        gutters: ['CodeMirror-linenumbers', 'CodeMirror-foldgutter'], // Folding type
      }}
    />
  )
};

Effect Display

Notes

The way of importing is a bit different in less files compared to jsx or tsx files.

  • In less files, import the theme file as follows
// style.less
@import '~codemirror/theme/eclipse.css';
  • Import as follows in *.jsx or *.tsx
import 'codemirror/theme/eclipse.css';

Reference

The CodeArea component is built on top of the CodeMirror component. For more editor configuration options, you can refer to the CodeMirror official website.
